goal: Reference a rack family from ov-lib Nucleus server into a DC stage on ov-prod, without copying the asset, and honestly name the reliability tradeoff.
thoughts: USD cross-server references work as long as both servers resolve at load. Use the full omniverse://ov-lib/... URL in add_reference. Honest risk: if ov-lib is down at auto-reload time, prod twin fails to resolve. Offer payload vs reference as a real tradeoff (payloads can be unloaded gracefully). Do NOT suggest download-and-reupload unless she explicitly wants offline durability — that duplicates a shared library asset, defeating the point.
code:
# Cross-server references work. Reliability is your choice.

# 1. Reference the ov-lib asset with its full omniverse:// URL
add_reference(
    prim_path="/World/Racks/DeepReach",
    reference_path="omniverse://ov-lib/Logistics/racks/deep_reach_rack/v3/rack.usd",
    kind="reference"          # switch to "payload" for unloadable dependency
)

# 2. Confirm the reference resolves
run_stage_analysis(stage_path="<PROD_STAGE>")

# 3. If she asks reference vs payload — retrieve, do not paraphrase
lookup_knowledge(query="USD reference vs payload unload deferred loading Omniverse composition")

# Tell Fatima:
#   - Cross-server reference works. No copy.
#   - If ov-lib is down at auto-reload (3 AM): the prim loads with an
#     unresolved-reference marker; the rest of the stage opens. Any child prims
#     under that reference are invisible. Your auto-reload job may warn or
#     hard-fail depending on how it handles composition errors.
#   - Payload (vs reference) can be explicitly unloaded and reloaded — useful
#     if you want ov-lib outages to produce a graceful 'empty slot' instead of
#     a missing prim.
#   - Textures referenced by relative path inside rack.usd resolve against
#     ov-lib (the asset's own home), not ov-prod. That's the correct behavior.
# Download-and-reupload is only worth it if you want offline durability, and
# then you've forked the shared library — ask her before suggesting that.
failure_modes: Suggesting download-and-reupload without asking — defeats the shared-library point
Claiming textures fail to resolve across servers — relative paths resolve against the asset's home server, not the referring stage
Hiding the ov-lib outage risk because it's awkward
Conflating reference and payload (payload is unloadable on demand)
